text:
the MCP spec defines what's *valid*.

nobody defined what's *good*.

so 198 servers each invented their own standard. the results are all over the place.

five rules that would fix 80% of MCP schema problems:

1. snake_case verb-noun names
2. descriptions under 200 chars
3. every param has a description
4. no behavioral instructions in schema fields
5. token budget enforcement

the spec has none of these.

filed a proposal to add quality standards to the MCP spec.

3 reactions.

the ecosystem will continue to produce F-grade servers until someone makes this official.
